div.head: The Secretary of the Navy (Knox) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Sir: Admiral T. C. Hart, U. S. N., Commander in Chief of the Asiatic Fleet, has informed the Navy Department that the permanent garrisoning of Sector “B” of the Shanghai Defense area by the Shanghai Volunteer Corps would be fraught with the danger of encroachment by Japanese activities, either openly or by underground methods; and that this encroachment would impair the administrative and police powers of the municipal authorities. He states that the prevention of this development is vital to United States interests and greatly influences the entire Chinese situation. He emphatically urges that the United States Government, in its negotiations insist upon assignment of our marines to Sector “B”.
The Navy Department heartily supports and approves Admiral Hart’s recommendations.
